about.title: About Godroox LLC
about.subtitle: Professional bookkeeping, trust compliance, and daily financial operations for real estate firms and brokerages in the US.
about.mission1: We prepare small and medium businesses to grow with financial integrity. Our goal is for every client to reach the stage where they have real options: continue as a family business, attract institutional investors, expand nationally, or one day go public through an IPO.
about.mission2: We don't promise growth. We guarantee that when the opportunity comes, your books are ready to be audited, your reports are GAAP compliant, and your financial operations won't be an obstacle.
about.step1: Initial diagnostic. We understand the current state of your books before promising anything.
about.step2: Cleanup if needed. If there are months or years of unorganized records, we clean before starting current operations.
about.step3: Continuous operation. Daily bookkeeping, reconciliation, audit, monthly reports.
about.step4: Strategic support. When you're ready to seek investment or IPO, we already have the foundations ready.

faq.title: FAQ
faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about GodManager

items.question: What is GodManager, exactly?
items.answer: GodManager is more than software. It's a complete service pairing human bookkeeping, a real-time management dashboard, and regulatory compliance — public blended pricing starts at $19.90 per property/month across our three plans.

items.question: Are you a SaaS company or a bookkeeping firm?
items.answer: We're both. We built our own platform so our bookkeepers can execute better work for you. You get tools and specialists for one price.

items.question: Who operates GodManager?
items.answer: Godroox LLC, headquartered in Florida. We carry professional E&O insurance at $1M (expandable to $3M), so covered errors committed by our team come with institutional protection.

items.question: Do you focus only on property management?
items.answer: Yes — that's our lane. We're fluent with Florida trust accounting requirements, deliver three-way reconciliation for brokerages/real-estate workloads, and keep 1099 timing on rails. We're not generalized bookkeepers.

items.question: How do you compare to AppFolio?
items.answer: AppFolio is software. You still fund an in-house bookkeeper, trust auditors, and your own workflows. GodManager bundles platform + bookkeeping + daily reconciliations + compliance together. For 100 properties: AppFolio + bookkeeper + auditor typically totals $4,400-$8,000/mo. GodManager = $1,990/mo total.

items.question: What if I already use AppFolio?
items.answer: Great — migration is smoother. We'll import/API-sync data while you decide whether AppFolio keeps running operations while GodManager owns the books, or everything routes through us.

items.question: Are you cheaper than an internal bookkeeper?
items.answer: Usually. Junior bookkeepers across Florida commonly cost $45,000-$60,000/year before benefits/stacked tools plus annual auditors ($2,000-$10,000). That lands around $50,000-$80,000/year fixed versus $23,880/year variable at 100 properties with GodManager ($19.90 blended average) — never taking PTO.

items.question: Why trust you versus another new firm?
items.answer: Three reasons: (1) Professional E&O insurance at $1M-$3M (most competitors do not carry it). (2) Proprietary platform integrated with human bookkeeping—we are not software-only. (3) Specialization in Florida property management (specific rules for trust accounting).

items.question: What exactly does pricing look like?
items.answer: Starting at $19.90 per property/month (blended average). Three different plans fit your footprint — still no per-transaction fees, forced minimum spend, or surprise upsells. Everything bundled: software, bookkeeping, reconciliations, trust compliance, 1099 filing, and concierge consulting.

items.question: Is there an annual plan with discounts?
items.answer: Yes. Agreements renew month-to-month — clients prepaid for twelve months upfront can negotiate a discount directly with Sales.

items.question: When do you start billing?
items.answer: Only after onboarding is 100% complete. Migration/setup runs up to fifteen days unpaid.

items.question: Can I cancel anytime?
items.answer: After the first 30 days (trial posture), cancellations require 30 days' written notice. You settle only for elapsed service.

items.question: How is invoicing handled?
items.answer: Monthly on the twentieth. Total equals your plan price × active subscribed properties on billing day.

items.question: Do you perform trust accounting?
items.answer: Absolutely — that's our speciality. Delivered through trust.godmanager.com with daily three-way reconciliation while staying aligned with Florida Real Estate Commission trust guidelines for IOLTA balances.

items.question: What happens if Florida's Real Estate Commission audits us?
items.answer: We already stage everything: documentation sets, archived daily reconciliations, per-owner reporting — you'll never stare down an examiner alone.

items.question: Do you prepare the 1099s for contractors?
items.answer: Yes. We collect payer data, file forms with the IRS plus contractors ahead of statutory deadlines — late-payment penalties aren't your problem when we quarterback compliance.

items.question: How long does onboarding take?
items.answer: Fifteen days end-to-end: AppFolio/Excel migrations, dashboard enablement, team training, and bank connectivity included.

items.question: Which access do you need to launch?
items.answer: Read-mostly portals for banking/AppFolio (if applicable), required APIs plus trust escrow portals — scoped under confidentiality agreement.

items.question: What if everything lives in spreadsheets?
items.answer: We migrate Excel cleanly; historical tagging may run longer billed as curated cleanup engagements.

items.question: Are my datasets secure?
items.answer: Yes — hosted on Railway in the USA, nightly backups, encryption in-flight/at-rest, confidentiality continuing three years beyond contract expiry.

items.question: Nearly $20 per property sounds expensive.
items.answer: Model it: one hundred doors equals $1,990/mo versus a $4,500-$5,500 bench bookkeeper, software ($400/mo), annual-audit cost blended to $2,500/mo, and E&O ($291/mo). Stack you're replacing runs $7,691-$8,691/mo — you pocket $5,701-$6,701 every month. Still expensive?

items.question: I already have a longstanding bookkeeper.
items.answer: Ask them: reconcile daily versus monthly — and carry their own E&O? Monthly plus no coverage means preventable exposure. Pilot GodManager concurrently for thirty days measured against their KPIs.

items.question: We're too slammed to migrate systems.
items.answer: That overload is precisely why spreadsheets slip. Migrating absorbs ~15 elapsed days on our side — you lend credentials once, then claw back hours weekly once automation lands.

items.question: Owners refuse to onboard something new.
items.answer: Owners keep receiving statements/disbursements the same outwardly — they'll simply notice sharper, faster artifacts without leaving their workflow.

items.question: I want proof before trusting you.
items.answer: Book a complementary 30-minute diagnostic onsite: live anonymized dashboards, stakeholder Q&A, zero obligation.
